title: More than two choices for "if" formula

posts.author: dara_t (Dara Thomason)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a formula that can show the one column out of five options that has the greatest number?

Screenshot 2024-10-29 at 4.15.32 PM1252×256 17 KB

In this case, I want the column that took the most days to be reported out in its own column.

Here is what I have tried, but I am missing something - I am admittedly not good with formulas:

IF({Days in PLANNING Phase}>{Days in CONCEPTING Phase},{Days in PRE-PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in DELIVERY Phase},“PLANNING”)

posts.author: skmdc (SK)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not very efficient formula but works (with one caveat – if one or more columns have same value then the first column from the left that was found to have the highest value will be reported)

IF({Days in PLANNING Phase} = MAX({Days in PLANNING Phase},{Days in CONCEPT Phase},{Days in PRE-PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in DELIVERY Phase}), "PLANNING",
 IF({Days in CONCEPT Phase} = MAX({Days in PLANNING Phase},{Days in CONCEPT Phase},{Days in PRE-PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in DELIVERY Phase}), "CONCEPT",
  IF({Days in PRE-PRODUCTION Phase} = MAX({Days in PLANNING Phase},{Days in CONCEPT Phase},{Days in PRE-PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in DELIVERY Phase}), "PRE-PROD",
   IF({Days in PRODUCTION Phase} = MAX({Days in PLANNING Phase},{Days in CONCEPT Phase},{Days in PRE-PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in PRODUCTION Phase},{Days in DELIVERY Phase}), "PROD",
   IF(OR(LEN({Days in DELIVERY Phase}) = 1, {Days in DELIVERY Phase} = 0),"---","DELIVERY"
    )
   )
  )
 )
)

posts.author: dara_t (Dara Thomason)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you SO much, this works perfectly! I really appreciate the help!
